description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation consisting of a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a single vertical pole. The solar panel array is composed of eight individual modules arranged in a 2x4 grid, each with a visible grid of solar cells. The panels are tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture and are framed with a metallic structure, likely aluminum or steel, for durability and stability. The supporting pole is anchored into a square base set on a patch of ground, simulating installation in an open outdoor area such as a park or roadside. No visible electrical cabling or inverter housing is present, and the mounting structure appears robust and weather-resistant. The overall design is typical for small-scale urban or suburban solar energy infrastructure, providing renewable power in public or semi-public spaces.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with eight modules on a single pole, tilted for optimal sunlight capture and set on a patch of grass. The structure is metallic and robust, suitable for outdoor urban environments.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the back of the solar panel array and the supporting pole anchored in the ground.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the single vertical support pole.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the full face of the 2x4 grid of solar panels and the supporting structure.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the panel tilt and the shadow cast on the ground.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (likely south), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground installation with metallic (likely steel or aluminum) support structure
attribute_array_configuration: 2x4 grid of solar panels in a single rectangular array
